# Speaker Notes Master

## Opening line

The internet does not allocate attention by truth. It allocates attention by signals. Those signals are useful until they become profitable to manufacture.

## Core transition

This is not a talk about “marketing is bad.” It is about the boundary where marketing stops improving discovery and starts falsifying the evidence of discovery.

## Thesis line

Manufactured traction corrupts the proxy signals that digital markets use to allocate attention, trust, money, and legitimacy.

## Closing line

When proof becomes synthetic, markets lose the ability to distinguish real demand from engineered appearance. The ethical alternative is not anti-growth; it is transparent growth built on real evidence.
